humanSummary.text: Audit found no P0/P1 blockers. The exact linker exposes a CLI and service surface, uses exact equality, does not fuzzy match or merge, and has fixture-backed positive and negative paths. Malformed JSON hardening and DB-backed service integration coverage are recorded as follow-ups.

findings.title: Malformed exact-linker CLI JSON handling
findings.description: The CLI casts parsed JSON to the exact-link request type and the service dereferences request fields without a top-level object guard. Valid negative fixture paths return semantic diagnostics, but malformed top-level JSON can throw instead of returning `catalog.exact_external_id.invalid_request`.

evidence.detail: Catalog audit identified direct unknown-to-request casting in the CLI and missing object guard in the linker service.

findings.impact: Malformed CLI inputs can bypass the intended semantic diagnostic surface and produce less stable process failures.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Malformed top-level JSON and non-object request payloads return `catalog.exact_external_id.invalid_request`.
CLI tests cover malformed JSON syntax, arrays, null, and scalar request payloads.

proposedDagNode.title: Exact-linker malformed JSON diagnostics
proposedDagNode.summary: Harden catalog exact-linker CLI and service request parsing so malformed top-level JSON returns semantic diagnostics.
deliverables: Runtime request guard for CatalogExactExternalIdLinkRequest payloads
CLI and service tests for malformed JSON, null, arrays, and scalar request payloads
acceptanceCriteria: Malformed top-level request payloads return stable `catalog.exact_external_id.invalid_request` diagnostics.
The exact-linker CLI never throws raw type errors for malformed request files.
auditFocus: Malformed exact-linker request payloads bypassing semantic diagnostics

findings.title: DB-backed exact-linker service integration test
findings.description: The linker service tests use a fake repository while the repository exact lookup is covered separately. A small DB-backed integration test would prove the service and real repository work together.

evidence.detail: Catalog audit found service behavior and repository exact lookup covered separately but not by a single DB-backed service integration test.

findings.impact: Future service/repository drift could pass isolated fake-repository tests and repository tests while breaking the composed linker path.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A DB-backed test seeds catalog works through ItotoriCatalogRepository and invokes ItotoriCatalogExactExternalIdLinkerService.
The integration test covers exact match and no-match behavior through the real repository.

proposedDagNode.title: Exact-linker DB service integration test
proposedDagNode.summary: Add a DB-backed exact-linker integration test that exercises the exported linker service against the real catalog repository.
deliverables: DB-backed exact-linker service integration test in packages/itotori-db/test
Fixture seed data covering exact match and no-match through ItotoriCatalogRepository
acceptanceCriteria: The exported linker service returns the same exact-match diagnostics against the real repository as it does against fake repository tests.
The test runs in the @itotori/db suite with DATABASE_URL.
auditFocus: Exact-linker service and repository composition drift
